content.full_text:
October 26, 1976

Ms. Melanese Loftin
120 Montgomery Street
San Francisco, California 94106

Dear Ms. Loftin:

This letter is to confirm my advice to you by telephone on
October 21, 1976, regarding the reporting obligations of major
donor committees under the Political Reform Act.

You asked whether persons who are major donor committees under
Government Code Section 82013(c) are required to report campaign
contributions made to candidates outside of California. I
stated that major donor committees must report only contributions
to and expenditures in behalf of candidates for California state
and local offices.

Sincerely,

NAM HAMBLETON
Special Compliance Representative

NH:mak

bcc: Jeanne

November 1, 1976


Ms. Melanie Loftin
120 Montgomery Street
San Francisco, California 94106

Dear Ms. Loftin:

This letter is to supplement the information contained in my
letter to you of October 26, 1976.

Under the Political Reform Act, major donor committees must report
only contributions and expenditures relative to California state
and local ballot measure campaigns.

Sincerely,


NAN HAMBLETON
Special Compliance Representative


NH:mak

bcc: Jeanne

sections.question: Whether persons who are major donor committees under Government Code Section 82013(c) are required to report campaign contributions made to candidates outside of California.
sections.conclusion: Major donor committees must report only contributions to and expenditures in behalf of candidates for California state and local offices. Under the Political Reform Act, major donor committees must report only contributions and expenditures relative to California state and local ballot measure campaigns.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked whether major donor committees must report contributions to candidates outside of California or only to California candidates and ballot measures.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Major donor committees are required to report only contributions to and expenditures for California state and local candidates and ballot measures, not contributions to candidates outside California.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document consists of two related letters from October 26 and November 1, 1976, both providing advice on major donor committee reporting obligations. The second letter supplements the first with additional clarification regarding ballot measure campaigns. Both letters are signed by NAN HAMBLETON, Special Compliance Representative.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether persons who are major donor committees under Government Code Section 82013(c) are required to report campaign contributions made to candidates outside of California.

CONCLUSION: Major donor committees must report only contributions to and expenditures in behalf of candidates for California state and local offices. Under the Political Reform Act, major donor committees must report only contributions and expenditures relative to California state and local ballot measure campaigns.

embedding.summary: Two related FPPC advice letters confirming that major donor committees under the Political Reform Act must report only contributions and expenditures for California state and local offices and ballot measures, not contributions to candidates outside California.
